sections.paragraph:
συνζ- s. συζ-.a

συνήγορος, ου, ὁ (Aeschyl., Demosth. et al.; ins; PAmh 23, 15; 24 [II b.c.]; POxy 1479, 5; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 121; Ar. 8, 4; Tat.1, 2; loanw. in rabb.) one who serves as an advocate in legal matters, attorney (Diod S 1, 76, 1; Philo, Vi. Cont. 44) ISm 5:1.—DELG s.v. ἀγορά 3.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 971
